title: Product Manager
jd_text:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.
Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.
Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.
The Search at eBay team is responsible for the search page that drives a large part of eBay’s core marketplace business. The Search team strives to build an experience that enables users to easily explore, find, and buy what they want at the best value through an engaging, frictionless, and trusted Search experience, helping them make confident purchase decisions.
In this role, you will be responsible to building high quality advertising solutions for Search. Your expertise will drive the development of AI-driven features that improve product discovery, engagement, and overall ad quality. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in AI/ML technologies, along with a passion for applying these skills to redefine the way customers interact with our platform.
What You Will Accomplish
Lead the product strategy and roadmap for improving advertising quality on search
Be a strategic problem solver and able to synthesize data from multiple sources (including analytics, user research, marketing research, and design) to identify key customer unmet needs, key solutions that can address the needs and build product vision with search item tiles
You will work closely with a cross-functional team of engineering, design, analytics, product marketing, and user research to deliver these products
You will lead the execution of your roadmap by working with data science, engineering, and analytics teams
Build MVPs, iterate fast and be the champion of execution excellence
Conduct AB tests for launch candidates and work with analytics teams to measure the impact on success metrics
What You Will Bring
3+ years of Product Management experience
Experience with search and discovery systems, including familiarity with ranking models, search algorithms, and semantic search
Familiarity with pay per click search advertising systems
Strong analytic skills and ability to synthesize data from multiple sources
Passionate advocate for user, and has confirmed results in achieving superb user experience within tight constraints
Demonstrated leadership ability, self-motivated and directed, entrepreneurial ability to innovate quickly
Additional Details
eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.
We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

The ideal candidate will have a strong background in AI/ML and experience with search and discovery systems. The candidate will lead the product strategy and roadmap, working closely with cross-functional teams to deliver products and drive execution excellence.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Product Manager (AI-Native Platform)
Location:
Berlin (Hybrid)
We're partnering with an exciting early-stage, venture-backed technology company building an AI-native platform that is transforming how complex operational workflows are managed and automated.
Backed by leading investors and operating at the forefront of AI, workflow automation and agentic systems, they are looking for a highly entrepreneurial Product Manager to join their growing team and work directly alongside the founders.
This is a unique opportunity for someone who thrives in ambiguity, enjoys speaking with customers, and wants to play a hands-on role in shaping both product strategy and execution.
The Opportunity
This is not a traditional roadmap management role.
We're looking for someone who can operate like a mini-founder; identifying customer problems, validating solutions, collaborating with engineers and designers, and driving products from idea through to launch.
You'll have significant ownership from day one and play a key role in helping shape the next generation of AI-powered workflows.
What You'll Be Doing
Product Discovery & Customer Research
Conduct customer interviews and discovery sessions
Deeply understand customer workflows, pain points and operational challenges
Translate customer insights into product opportunities
Validate assumptions before development begins
Product Strategy & Prioritisation
Work closely with founders to define product priorities
Evaluate opportunities based on customer impact and business value
Contribute to product strategy and roadmap decisions
Balance immediate customer needs with long-term product vision
Product Delivery
Collaborate closely with Engineering and Design teams
Define product requirements and acceptance criteria
Drive initiatives from concept through to launch
Ensure alignment across stakeholders and teams
AI-Native Product Development
Identify opportunities where AI can automate workflows and improve user experiences
Collaborate on AI-powered features, automation tools and agentic systems
Define success metrics and evaluate outcomes
Leverage AI tools to accelerate discovery, decision-making and execution
Analytics & Optimisation
Define and track product success metrics
Analyse user behaviour and product adoption
Identify opportunities to improve activation, engagement and retention
Drive continuous product improvements through data and customer feedback
What they are looking For
They are intentionally open-minded on background.
You may currently be:
A Product Manager
A Product Designer who has moved into Product
A Software Engineer who has transitioned into Product
A Startup Founder or Operator with strong product instincts
More important than job title is your ability to identify problems, work closely with customers and ship great products.
Must Have
3-5 years of relevant experience in Product Management, Product Design, Software Engineering or a similar role
Experience building and shipping digital products
Strong customer discovery and research skills
Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
Strong ownership mentality and startup mindset
Ability to operate autonomously in fast-moving environments
Experience working closely with Engineering and Design teams
We Nice to Have
B2B SaaS experience
Early-stage startup experience
Product Design background
Software Engineering background
Experience with AI products, automation platforms or workflow tools
Experience working directly with founders
German language skills
Who Will Thrive Here
Builders, not coordinators
Customer-obsessed product thinkers
Individuals who move quickly and learn through iteration
People who embrace ownership and accountability
Those who are excited by AI and emerging technologies
Strong communicators who can simplify complexity
Curious, low-ego and highly collaborative team players
Why Join?
Work directly with founders and senior leadership
Significant ownership and impact from day one
Shape an emerging AI-nati

The ideal candidate will have 3-5 years of experience in product management, strong customer discovery skills, and the ability to operate autonomously in a fast-moving environment. The company is looking for a highly entrepreneurial individual who can drive products from idea to launch and shape the next generation of AI-powered workflows.

title: Senior Product Manager
jd_text:
Immowelt is a leading German digital real estate marketplace and part of the Aviv Group, a European family of real estate platforms across Germany, France, and Belgium, building trusted property ecosystems.
THE ROLE
We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to own a key part of the Professional, agent-facing side of the Immowelt marketplace, with a specific focus on lead management, agent messaging, and our professional mobile tooling to be launched later this year.
Leads are our platform’s core value-creation activity, representing user inquiries on real estate listings.
You will own the experience determining how agents manage leads at their desks or on the move. This high-impact role involves user discovery, data-driven decision-making, and migrating agents to a modern, consolidated toolset.
You will report directly to the Product Director and work closely with a cross-functional product team. You will also play a central role in a major programme to deprecate our legacy products and migrate agents to a modern, consolidated toolset.
We need a strong mid-level PM ready to grow, offering real responsibility and a clear path to greater scope.
YOUR MISSION
Consolidate the agent workspace for managing leads by:
Executing the Agent Leads Hub roadmap for managing message enquiries and call tracking and live chat.
Leading the migration from legacy tools to modern workflows, retiring technical debt.
Scoping instant messaging and document sharing for Pro customers, get us to a go/no go decision for this functionality
Shaping the Immowelt GO mobile app (iOS/Android) featuring quality scoring, auto-responders, and chat for a late 2026 launch.
Conducting regular user discovery via site visits and data analysis to guide roadmap decisions.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Product Discovery & Strategy
Run dual-track discovery to maintain an evidenced backlog.
Synthesise research, behavioural data, and sales feedback into a prioritised roadmap.
Define measurable goals and north-star metrics for all initiatives.
Validate opportunities through fast experimentation and iterative shipping.
Delivery & Execution
Own end-to-end delivery in partnership with engineering and design.
Engage in architecture discussions and build vs. buy decisions.
Drive legacy deprecation with clear migration plans and communication.
Ensure mobile quality across iOS and Android agent experiences.
Data & Experimentation
Define instrumentation requirements for full product measurability.
Query data independently to validate hypotheses and monitor performance.
Focus on outcome-based metrics that drive agent and business value.
Stakeholder Communication
Align stakeholders from engineering to senior leadership on strategy and progress.
Present roadmap updates to leadership using strategic context and storytelling.
Collaborate with account managers to surface pain points and close feedback loops.
WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR
Experience & Background
3+ years as a PM, ideally in B2B SaaS, marketplaces, or PropTech.
Proven impact from discovery through delivery in agile squads.
Experience working in cross-functional agile squads, with genuine ownership – not just backlog management.
Familiarity with dual-track discovery and delivery methodologies.
Experience with iOS/Android mobile product development is a plus.
Data & Technical Skills
Comfortable validating assumptions and monitoring product health through data.
Technical fluency to discuss APIs and delivery trade-offs with engineers.
Experience with A/B testing and validation tools.
Proficient with AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.) and prototyping (Claude Code, Replit, Lovable) is a plus.
Communication & Mindset
Structured communicator effective with technical and non-technical audiences.
Action-oriented with an ownership mindset and comfort with ambiguity.
Language & Location
Must speak & write German at B2 level or above (native speaker preferred). This is a customer-facing product role where market and linguistic understanding matters.
Strong

The role involves user discovery, data-driven decision-making, and migrating agents to a modern toolset. title: Senior Product Manager, Exchange
jd_text:
Who We Are
Cint is a pioneer in research technology (ResTech). Our customers use the Cint platform to post questions and get answers from real people to build business strategies, confidently publish research, accurately measure the impact of digital advertising, and more. The Cint platform is built on a programmatic marketplace, which is the world’s largest, with nearly 300 million respondents in over 130 countries.
At Cint, we are committed to innovation and are rapidly expanding our portfolio of data solutions to power the next generation of insights. We are looking for passionate, results-oriented leaders to join us on this journey.
Job Description
The Role
Join our Product Team as a Senior Product Manager. You’ll craft the strategic roadmap and steer the delivery of our Demand services. This role, based in the EU/UK, requires collaboration with our US teams during CET hours and can be remote or attached to one of our existing engineering offices.
We're on the hunt for skilled product managers who have a unique blend of deep technical understanding, customer obsession and a love for solving product problems. With countless potential features to bring to life in Cint, seize the opportunity to be the decision-maker on one of the most impactful domains within Cint.
What You Will Do
Own the Vision: Develop and promote a product vision for your part of Cint’s Exchange. We make long term investments with large payoffs for success, see the future and articulate it.
Tell the Story: Masterfully articulate the "why" behind your product strategy. Bring complex customer and technical problems to life, inspiring and aligning senior leadership and the broader organization around your vision.
Execute with Excellence: Conduct thorough opportunity assessments, backed by data and customer research, to validate assumptions and prioritize initiatives. Author clear, comprehensive product requirements and partner with R&D leadership to navigate complex technical trade-offs.
Partner to Win: Build strong, trusted relationships with key stakeholders across R&D, Data Science, Commercial, Operations, Legal, and Marketing to ensure cross-functional alignment and a successful go-to-market.
Qualifications
What We Are Looking For
Proven Product Operator: You have a proven track record of launching, operating and scaling complex products, platforms, or services from concept to market or transforming existing products for their next stage of growth.
Customer-Obsessed: You are an expert in translating customer problems from the ResTech and data space into clear, well-structured product requirements, backed by data and qualitative insights.
Technical Expertise: You have direct experience managing the full lifecycle of API and UI driven products and can also clearly demonstrate shipping high quality experiences. Demonstrated knowledge, experience and interest in how LLMs and modern coding stacks challenge long held verities would be a strong plus.
Exceptional Communicator: You possess outstanding written and verbal communication skills and are seen as an expert storyteller. You can effectively partner with senior leadership and are skilled at managing complex stakeholder maps.
Remote First: Ability to work independently in a remote-first environment.
Why Join Us?
Strategic Impact: You will be driving the foundation of Cint's current and future success.
Innovation: You will be on the cutting edge of ResTech and data technology, solving novel problems with a world-class R&D team.
Global Reach: Cint is a global company with a distributed, flexible, and collaborative culture.
Ownership: We empower our product team. You will have the autonomy and accountability to make a measurable impact on the business.
Additional Information
Our Values
Collaboration is our superpower
We uncover rich perspectives across the world
Success happens together
We deliver across borders.
Innovation is in our blood
We’re pioneers in our industry
Our curiosity is insatiable

Creating high-quality, on-brand charts, visuals, and business graphics used to be hard. Vizzlo changes that. We offer the only tool on the market that integrates with Google Slides, PowerPoint, and more. title: Founding Product Manager
jd_text:
Why we are doing this
Hospital care in Germany is under structural strain.
Two thirds of hospitals operate at a loss, and most are stretched in staff, beds, and budgets. At the same time, modern surgery has moved on — many procedures that still mean an overnight stay no longer medically require one. The result: patients stay longer than they need to, infrastructure carries avoidable load, and the system absorbs costs it could avoid.
We're building a new generation of ambulatory surgery centres in Germany to change this.
Software-driven from day one. Designed to get people back to their lives faster, and to make outpatient surgery the default — not the exception.
Healthcare is a complex space. We are set up to make a difference:
Our leadership team has built and scaled 5+ successful ventures; we are backed by top tech and healthcare investors; and with multiple clinical partners onboard we are ready to move fast.
What you’ll do
You take product ownership of TokuraOS
—
the “orchestration engine” that sits on top of existing clinic stacks, orchestrating the entire care pathway end to end
, from patient intake to surgery, recovery, and daily operations, connecting digital patient journeys, on-site infrastructure, and a unified data layer into a production-grade platform.
Greenfield. Built to work when a surgeon is standing in the operating room. Built to scale across 100+ clinics.
What the role offers
First product hire.
Clear path to building, shaping and leading the product organisation alongside our CPTO. A rare opportunity to define a mission-critical product from ground-up.
Real ownership from day 1.
You shape the vision, define the roadmap, own how the product behaves in real clinic environments, and drive the end user change management. You'll split your time between product development — deciding what we build, for whom, and why it matters — and hands-on field research in and out of the OR.
Strategic freedom by design.
We have a strong hypothesis on what we need to build when, but the final product roadmap is intentionally open, to be co-shaped with you.
“Full-stack” in the truest sense.
We're not
just
building a technical product — we're building and operating physical clinics; harder than a pure software play, but far more impactful.
Small, top-tier, high-trust team.
You’ll work side by side with our CPTO (serial tech founder (YCombinator), ex-McK) and an experienced founder-duo that has built and scaled multiple hyper-growth startups to 400+ employees.
What you bring
Essential
You've shaped products with meaningful usage in complex, real-world environments.
The ability to translate the messy reality of a care journey into an experience that feels
genuinely
different — for patients, providers, and the broader care team. From intake to post-op recovery: every touchpoint is yours to understand deeply and shape optimally.
You are a strategic thinker, bring structure to any analysis, have an eye for design. You are comfortable in the space where product meets tech.
A strong pull toward learning. Trajectory matters more than credentials and experience.
Structured thinking, clear communication. You build trust quickly, whether you're with a surgeon, a nurse, or a patient.
High ownership, reliability, and a bias toward shipping with fast feedback loops.
Bonus
Prior experience working with sensitive data and/ or in regulated environments where compliance and data protection are design constraints, not afterthoughts.
Healthcare/ healthtech background.
Logistics
Berlin. On-site. Start ASAP.
Attractive compensation with meaningful equity upside.
Professional German required.
We value judgment over process, clarity over complexity, and systems that work over systems that impress.
We believe the best products emerge when everyone understands the real environments their work serves and takes ownership of what we design, build, and ship.
If this resonates, let’s talk.
summary: The role is for a product owner to lead the development of TokuraOS, an orchestration engine for ambulatory surgery centers, with a focus on shaping the product vision, defining the roadmap, and driving user change management. The ideal candidate will have experience in shaping products for complex environments and be a strategic thinker with an eye for design. The company is looking for someone to join their team in Berlin, with a strong pull toward learning and a bias toward shipping with fast feedback loops.

title: Product Owner (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Über uns
Conlivo ist ein PropTech-Unternehmen aus Hamburg.
Wir betreiben eine cloud-native Plattform für die Energie- und Verbrauchsabrechnung in der Immobilienwirtschaft. Unsere Software bildet die technischen und regulatorischen Abläufe rund um Heizung, Messung und Verbrauchsabrechnung digital ab und automatisiert so weite Teile der Abrechnung.
Zur Weiterentwicklung unseres Produkts suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt Verstärkung als Product Owner (m/w/d).
Wen wir suchen:
Die Rolle als Product Owner (m/w/d) erfordert sowohl ein fachliches Verständnis von Gebäude- und Heizungstechnik als auch einen souveränen Umgang mit Softwareentwicklung.
Wir wissen, dass dies ein besonderes Anforderungsprofil ist und kaum jemand beide Welten von Anfang an perfekt vereint. Wir freuen uns über Bewerbungen aus beiden Richtungen:
Aus der Software kommend:
Du kennst dich mit der Softwareentwicklung aus und hast bereits Erfahrung mit den Aufgaben eines Product Owners. Du hast ein Studium in Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder einem verwandten Feld und Lust, dich tief in die technische Domäne der Gebäude- und Heizungstechnik einzuarbeiten.
Aus dem Ingenieurwesen kommend:
Du hast einen Abschluss im Bauingenieurwesen, Maschinenbau oder einer vergleichbaren Disziplin (idealerweise mit Bezug zu Heizungs-, Versorgungs- oder Gebäudetechnik) und möchtest dich in Richtung Product Ownership entwickeln.
Entscheidend ist die Bereitschaft, in die jeweils andere Welt hineinzuwachsen – hierbei unterstützen wir dich natürlich.
Deine Aufgaben
Du verantwortest das Product Backlog und übersetzt fachliche und regulatorische Anforderungen aus der Gebäude- und Heizungstechnik in klare, umsetzbare Tickets.
Du priorisierst Features nach Kunden- und Geschäftswert, pflegst das Backlog und sicherst die Anforderungsqualität (User Stories, Akzeptanzkriterien, Definition of Done).
Als Schnittstelle zwischen Kunden, Stakeholdern und Entwicklung begleitest du die agilen Prozesse, planst Releases und entwickelst die Produkt-Roadmap weiter.
Dein Profil
Du bringst ein abgeschlossenes Studium mit – entweder mit informationstechnischem oder mit ingenieurwissenschaftlichem Schwerpunkt – sowie ein gutes technisches Verständnis von Softwareentwicklung. Software darf dir nicht gänzlich fremd sein.
Du hast Interesse und die Fähigkeit, dir entweder fachliches Wissen in Gebäude- und Heizungstechnik oder die Methodik der Product-Owner-Arbeit anzueignen.
Erfahrung als Product Owner / Product Manager, Kenntnisse agiler Methoden (Scrum/Kanban) sowie Bezug zur Energie- oder Immobilienwirtschaft sind willkommen, aber kein Muss.
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1) sowie ausgeprägte Kommunikations- und Moderationsfähigkeiten solltest du mitbringen.
Wir bieten
eine verantwortungsvolle Schlüsselrolle an der Schnittstelle von Ingenieurwesen und Software
großen Gestaltungsspielraum
flache Hierarchien
flexible und hybride Arbeitsmodelle

title: Founding Product Manager - KI-Agenten-Plattform (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Das Problem
Mittelständische Unternehmen treffen jeden Tag hunderte kleine Entscheidungen. Freigaben, Zuordnungen, Nachverfolgungen. Einzeln nicht komplex, aber in Summe fressen sie ganze Arbeitstage. Klassische Automatisierung hilft nur bei dem, was regelbasiert ist. Der schwierige Teil ist das menschliche Urteilsvermögen.
Wir bauen eine eigene Plattform, die genau dieses Problem löst: mit KI-Agenten, die operative Entscheidungen eigenständig übernehmen können. Wir wollen KI in den Mittelstand bringen, die Grenzen des Möglichen ausreizen und deutsche Unternehmen zukunftsfähig machen. Wir arbeiten direkt mit Unternehmern, die ihr Geschäft ernst meinen.
Das Produkt existiert, wird aktiv bei zahlenden Kunden eingesetzt und weiterentwickelt. Jetzt suchen wir die Person, die daraus ein eigenständig skalierendes Produkt macht. Als erster Produkthire.
Aufgaben
Die Rolle
Wir sind an dem Punkt, an dem aus einzelnen Kundenlösungen ein eigenständiges Produkt werden will. Diese Rolle entscheidet mit, wie das aussieht.
Wir sind ein kleines Team. Keine großen Abstimmungsschleifen, kein Ticket-Pingpong. Du bekommst Kontext, triffst Entscheidungen und siehst den Impact Deiner Arbeit innerhalb von Tagen.
Marek hat Lucid Labs gegründet, vorher Mitgründer von Flightright (0 auf 200+ Mitarbeiter, einer der erfolgreichsten Legal-Tech-Ventures Europas). Kurze Wege zu allen Entscheidungen, viel Eigenverantwortung. Du gestaltest, wie Produkt bei Lucid Labs funktioniert.
Die zwei Hälften
60% Customer Development.
Unsere zahlenden Kunden sind gleichzeitig unser Discovery-Engine. Du führst die Beziehungen zu 4–6 strategischen Design Partnern auf CEO/COO-Ebene. Nicht als Delivery Manager, sondern als PM, der Produktthesen direkt am lebenden Objekt validiert. Engineering steuerst Du über klare Outcomes, nicht über Tickets. Unsere Agenten liefern bei Kunden heute schon messbare Einsparungen im sechsstelligen Bereich pro Jahr.
40% Produktstrategie & Plattformaufbau.
Du verantwortest, was aus der Plattform wird: Positionierung, Featureschnitte, Verpackung. Du baust den Backlog, beobachtest den Wettbewerb, entwickelst alle zwei Wochen neue Demos und entscheidest, welche Einzellösung ein Modul wird und welche nicht.
Die zentrale Frage: Welche Features schaffen echten Wert? Wie wird aus einzelnen Kundenanforderungen ein skalierbares Produkt?
Unser Modell
Wir bauen tiefe Implementierung beim Kunden und eigenes Produkt. Diese Kombination ist unser Vorsprung: Was wir bei Kunden lernen, fließt direkt in die Plattform. Was wir an der Plattform bauen, macht uns beim nächsten Kunden besser.
Wie groß das in 24 Monaten wird, hängt davon ab, was wir gemeinsam daraus machen. Klar ist: Die Plattform wächst aus echten Kundenproblemen, nicht aus Strategie-Workshops.
Wir suchen Leute, die Lust haben, diese Reise mitzugestalten.
Wie wir Produkt bauen
Wir starten nicht mit einer Idee und validieren sie irgendwann. Wir starten beim Kunden, in echten Projekten. Wir sehen, wo es hakt. Wir bauen eine Lösung. Dann schauen wir: Taucht das gleiche Problem beim nächsten Kunden wieder auf? Wenn ja, wird aus der Einzellösung ein Feature. Aus dem Feature ein Modul. Aus dem Modul ein Teil der Plattform.
Realität, Lösung, Impact, Skalierung.
Beispiel: Letzte Woche merken wir bei einer Workshop-Vorbereitung, dass ein Teil unserer Methodik nicht weit genug trägt. Mittwoch, 3 Stunden Arbeit: Neue Version gebaut. Donnerstagmorgen, 9 Uhr: Läuft live beim Kunden. Im Workshop merken wir, wo es noch hakt. Am nächsten Tag ist die nächste Iteration fertig.
Der Abstand zwischen Idee, Umsetzung und Validierung beträgt bei uns Tage, nicht Monate.
Die schwierige Frage ist dabei immer dieselbe: Ist das, was wir gebaut haben, ein cleverer Hack für einen Kunden, oder der Kern von etwas, das für 50 Kunden funktioniert? Diese Entscheidung richtig zu treffen, ist Kern des Jobs.
Qualifikation
Was Du mitbringst
Du hast schon mindestens einmal ein B2B-Produkt von null auf echte Traction gebracht. Als Founding PM

title: Product Lead - KI-Agenten & Plattform (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Das Problem
Mittelständische Unternehmen treffen jeden Tag hunderte kleine Entscheidungen — Freigaben, Zuordnungen, Nachverfolgungen. Einzeln nicht komplex, aber in Summe fressen sie ganze Arbeitstage. Klassische Automatisierung hilft nur bei dem, was regelbasiert ist. Der schwierige Teil ist das menschliche Urteilsvermögen.
Wir bauen eine eigene Plattform, die genau dieses Problem löst — mit KI-Agenten, die operative Entscheidungen eigenständig übernehmen können, ein Produkt mit klarer Haltung, geschärft an dutzenden echten Kundenprojekten.
Das Produkt existiert, wird aktiv bei Kunden eingesetzt und weiterentwickelt. Jetzt suchen wir die Person, die es auf das nächste Level bringt — als erster Product Lead für unser eigenes Produkt.
Die Rolle
Das Produkt funktioniert, die ersten Kunden zahlen, die Nachfrage wächst. Jetzt geht es darum, daraus ein eigenständiges und skalierbares Produkt zu machen - von 1 auf 10, hands-on.
Du arbeitest direkt mit dem Gründer und einem wachsenden Engineering- und AI-Berateam, die Agenten bei Kunden implementiern. Keine großen Abstimmungsschleifen, kein Ticket-Pingpong. Du bekommst Kontext, triffst Entscheidungen und siehst den Impact deiner Arbeit innerhalb von Tagen - auch weil unsere Kunden die Plattform aktiv in den Projekten nutzen und du echtes Feedback direkt bekommst.
Die Rolle hat zwei Teile, die sich gegenseitig befruchten:
60% direkte Kundenarbeit:
Du bist eigenverantwortlicher Ansprechpartner für deine Kunden — wir arbeiten meist direkt mit der Geschäftsführung, auf Augenhöhe. Du managst mehrere Kundenprojekte gleichzeitig — von der Discovery über die Roadmap bis zur laufenden Delivery. Du sitzt in den zweiwöchentlichen Check-ins, übersetzt Geschäftsziele in klare Anforderungen, steuerst das Engineering-Team und sorgst dafür, dass die KI-Agenten, die wir bauen, echten Impact liefern — unsere Agenten sparen Kunden heute schon Hunderttausende Euro im Jahr. Du identifizierst neue Potenziale für den Einsatz von Agenten und bringst sie eigenständig ins Gespräch.
40% Eigenes Produkt:
Du übernimmst die Ownership für die Weiterentwicklung unserer Plattform. Du identifizierst aus den Kundenprojekten wiederkehrenden Bedarf, entwickelst den Backlog, analysierst den Wettbewerb und entwickelst regelmäßig neue Feature-Demos. Das Spannende: Jedes Kundenprojekt und jeder Kundenworkshop ist gleichzeitig ein User-Test für unsere Software.
Das Interessante an dieser Rolle ist nicht Stakeholder-Management — es ist die Frage: Welche Features schaffen echten Wert? Wie wird aus einem manuellen Beratungsprozess ein skalierbares Produkt? Wie entwickeln wir eine wertvolle Plattform, für die Kunden unabhängig von der individuellen Agenten-Entwicklung bezahlen?
Kurz gesagt:
Du machst aus funktionierenden Einzellösungen ein Produkt, das skaliert — und für das Kunden bereit sind, dauerhaft zu zahlen.
Aufgaben
Dein Arbeitsalltag
Mehrere Kundenprojekte parallel managen — Backlog planen, priorisieren, zweiwöchentliche Check-ins mit Kunden eigenständig leiten
Workshops mit Kunden vorbereiten, planen und durchführen — von der Discovery bis zum laufenden Scoping

Anforderungen in konkrete Lösungen übersetzen — auch mal selbst einen Claude Skill oder Prototypen bauen
Neue Automatisierungspotenziale beim Kunden entdecken und in Business Cases übersetzen
Die eigene Plattform weiterentwickeln — von der Idee über den Prototyp bis zur Demo (alle 2 Wochen Fortschritte präsentieren)
Wettbewerbslandschaft beobachten und neue Inspiration mitbringen
Einen Delivery-Prozess für die Agenten-Entwicklung mitgestalten, der mit 10+ Entwicklern und vielen parallelen Kundenprojekten skaliert
Deine ersten Wochen
In den ersten zwei Wochen tauchst du in unsere Kundenprojekte und die Plattform ein, challengst Annahmen und lernst unseren Beratungsprozess kennen. Nach sechs Wochen managst du eigenständig die ersten Kundenprojekte und hast Features zur Plattform beig

The role involves 60% customer work and 40% product development, with a focus on creating a scalable product that delivers value to customers. jd_translated:
The Problem
Medium-sized companies make hundreds of small decisions every day — approvals, assignments, tracking. Individually, they are not complex, but collectively, they consume entire working days. Classic automation only helps with rule-based processes. The difficult part is human judgment.
We are building our own platform that solves exactly this problem — with AI agents that can take over operational decisions independently, a product with a clear stance, refined through dozens of real customer projects.
The product exists, is actively used by customers, and is being further developed. Now we are looking for the person who will take it to the next level — as the first Product Lead for our own product.

The Role
The product works, the first customers pay, and demand is growing. Now it's about turning it into a standalone and scalable product - from 1 to 10, hands-on.
You will work directly with the founder and a growing engineering and AI consulting team that implements agents for customers. No lengthy approval loops, no ticket ping-pong. You will receive context, make decisions, and see the impact of your work within days - also because our customers actively use the platform in their projects and you receive real feedback directly.

The role has two parts that complement each other:
60% direct customer work:
You are the independent point of contact for your customers — we usually work directly with the management, at eye level. You manage multiple customer projects simultaneously — from discovery to roadmap to ongoing delivery. You participate in bi-weekly check-ins, translate business goals into clear requirements, steer the engineering team, and ensure that the AI agents we build deliver real impact — our agents already save customers hundreds of thousands of euros per year. You identify new potential for agent deployment and bring it into the conversation independently.

40% Own product:
You take ownership of the further development of our platform. You identify recurring needs from customer projects, develop the backlog, analyze the competition, and regularly develop new feature demos. The exciting thing: every customer project and every customer workshop is simultaneously a user test for our software.

The interesting thing about this role is not stakeholder management — it's the question: which features create real value? How does a manual consulting process become a scalable product? How do we develop a valuable platform that customers are willing to pay for, regardless of individual agent development?

In short:
You turn functioning individual solutions into a product that scales — and for which customers are willing to pay permanently.

Tasks
Your daily work
Manage multiple customer projects in parallel — plan and prioritize the backlog, lead bi-weekly check-ins with customers independently
Prepare, plan, and conduct workshops with customers — from discovery to ongoing scoping
Translate business goals into clear requirements and steer the engineering team
Translate requirements into concrete solutions — sometimes even building a Claude skill or prototype yourself
Discover new automation potential at the customer and translate it into business cases
Further develop the platform — from idea to prototype to demo (present progress every 2 weeks)
Observe the competitive landscape and bring new inspiration
Help shape a delivery process for agent development that scales with 10+ developers and many parallel customer projects

Your first weeks
In the first two weeks, you will dive into our customer projects and the platform, challenge assumptions, and get to know our consulting process. After six weeks, you will independently manage the first customer projects and have added features to the platform.

title: Product Owner (m/f/d) Digital Products & AI
jd_text:
Deine Aufgaben
Together, we set the standard for modern board decisions.
Werde Teil unserer Mission, die Governance-Infrastruktur von Vorstandsbüros weltweit neu zu definieren und entscheidend voranzutreiben – und setze Maßstäbe, die Organisationen nachhaltig prägen.
Als Product Owner (m/w/d) verantwortest Du die operative Steuerung und kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung unseres Softwareproduktes Boardwise im agilen Umfeld. Übersetzung von Anforderungen aus Fachbereichen und Stakeholdern in klare User Stories und Akzeptanzkriterien

Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Entwicklungsteams, insbesondere im Microsoft-Ökosystem (z. B. M365, Azure)
Aktive Steuerung und Moderation von Stakeholdern auf verschiedenen Unternehmensebenen
Unterstützung beider Definition und Weiterentwicklung der Produktvision und Roadmap
Integration und Weiterentwicklung von AI-basierten Funktionen im Produkt (z. B. Copilot, LLM-basierteFeatures)
Zusammenarbeit mit UX,Data und Engineering zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung der Nutzer-erfahrung
Sicherstellung von Qualität, Usability und Business Impact der umgesetzten Features
Dein Profil
Standortin Deutschland
Mehrjährige Erfahrung als Product Owner, Product Manager oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle im Softwareumfeld
Arbeiten im agilen Umfeld (Scrum, Kanban oder vergleichbar) ist für Dich selbstverständlich
Erfahrung in der Steuerung und Priorisierung von Backlogs
Know-how im Microsoft-Umfeld (M365, Azure, Microsoft-basierteAnwendungsentwicklung)
Fähigkeit, fachliche Anforderungen in klare, technisch umsetzbare Arbeitspakete zu übersetzen
Erfahrung im Stakeholder-Management, inkl. souveränem Auftreten auf Management-bzw.Vorstandsebene
Sehr gute Kommunikationsfähigkeiten und Stakeholder Management in Deutsch(C2) und Englisch
Warum wir?
100 % Remote: Arbeite von überall in Europa, mit Vertrauen, Flexibilität und Ownership.
Wachstumschancen: Entwickle dich weiter, ob in strategischen Rollen oder als Führungskraft.
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit dem Management
Teamspirit trotz Remote: Wir treffen uns mindestens 2x im Jahr, zum Beispiel in Lissabon, Berlin oder Kopenhagen.
Über uns
Boardwise wurde 2019 innerhalb von Axel Springer mit der Mission gegründet, Boardroom-Prozesse zu digitalisieren und zu vereinfachen. Was ursprünglich als internes Projekt begann, hat sich inzwischen zu einem schnell wachsenden, unabhängigen SaaS-Unternehmen entwickelt, geführt von einem erfahrenen Gründerteam mit starkem Hintergrund in Start-ups und Enterprise-Software.
Wir unterstützen Führungsteams und Gremienbüros großer Organisationen dabei, Meetings und Entscheidungsprozesse effizienter, sicherer und transparenter zu gestalten. Unsere Enterprise-Lösung wird bereits von zahlreichen namhaften Unternehmen genutzt – darunter mehr als 30 % der DAX-Konzerne, wie E.ON, Bayer, Volkswagen Financial Services und ProSiebenSat.1 Media.
Bei Boardwise entwickeln wir nicht nur Software – wir gestalten neu, wie strategische Entscheidungen in modernen Organisationen getroffen werden. Als Remote-first-Unternehmen mit einem internationalen Team bieten wir ein hohes Maß an Eigenverantwortung, steile Lernkurven und die Möglichkeit, in einem relevanten Umfeld echten Impact zu erzielen.

title: Product
jd_text:
Few people get to build a bank from scratch. Even fewer get to build one at a moment when banking itself is being reinvented.
The dollar is the best product in the history of the world, with practically infinite global demand, but distribution is broken. Global clearing runs on legacy banks that are closed 115 days a year, built for humans and take two days to settle. Augustus is the update to the internet era. We received conditional approval from the OCC to charter America’s first stablecoin-native clearing bank that is always open, made for machines, at the speed of compute.
We are a group of operators, unicorn early employees, ex-founders and people who walked away from degrees because they believe this is an industry and career defining opportunity. We're backed with $40M from Peter Thiel’s Valar Ventures, Creandum and founders of Ramp, Deel and Circle.
We are regulated in Europe and live with Euro and Stablecoin clearing today. Now, we are building the US team from the ground up, while continuing to grow rapidly in Europe.
The Role
You own the heart of the programmable bank: Payments and Accounts. The full global payment stack - US rails (ACH, FedWire, FedNow), international Swift, European schemes (SEPA, SEPA Instant, FPS), and the programmable accounts layer: DDAs, FBOs, and virtual accounts. Each is both a technical layer and a commercial product.
The end state: enterprise customers plug into Augustus via API and move money anywhere in the world, instantly, 24/7. Your job is to challenge the status quo - to understand what's possible on these rails and push further. Every product you ship should offer our customers something they've never been able to get from a bank before.
You'll work on Marble, our proprietary bank core. Your product decisions directly shape what robust, scalable, and fast means in practice. You'll get deep into the use cases our customers are building: how a stablecoin operator structures flows, how an OTC desk runs settlement, how a correspondent bank thinks about reconciliation. That understanding lets you work with design and engineering to develop our platform to uniquely solve for our customers needs.
You report to Will, Head of Product, and work closely with Simon, our CTO. You're expected to operate with full autonomy on your team's roadmap.
Your First 6 Months
Months 1-2: You take over day-to-day ownership of your product team - the roadmap, the ways of working, the hard decisions. You establish yourself with customers and key partners, getting deep on what they're building and where we're falling short. You define and ship your first project.
Months 3-4: Full execution mode. You're unblocking engineering daily, making hard prioritisation calls, and keeping the roadmap moving. You're spending time with customers, feeding what you learn straight back into the product, and already shaping the mid- to long-term roadmap.
Months 5-6: You're working with live customers on the products you've built, and deposit and payment volumes are scaling. You've moved from execution to platform mode - evolving the roadmap based on what your first customers are actually building on top of Augustus, and pushing the product further than any of them expected a bank could go.
Who Will Thrive At Augustus
We believe throwing smart people with high agency at big problems produces the best outcomes. The people who succeed here share the following traits:
Relentless: You can't leave something broken. You don't stop because it got hard or because no one is watching.
Set the bar: You're harder on yourself than anyone else would be. You don't need external accountability to care about quality.
Shape the Game: You don't wait for a playbook and you don't need one. You walk into genuinely new territory and figure it out. You move before anyone asked you to.
Systems first: You don't solve problems by adding people or effort. You build systems that make the problem smaller. Your first instinct is to automate, not to handle it

The role requires autonomy, relentless drive, and the ability to shape the game in a new territory.

title: Hardware Product Manager:in (w/m/d)
jd_text:
Unser Ziel ist eine Solaranlage auf jedem Dach, ein Speicher in jedem Haus und ein E-Auto in jeder Garage. Enpal macht das möglich mit einer integrierten Gesamtlösung für dezentrale Energie – von Solaranlage und Batteriespeicher über Wallbox, Smart Meter bis zur Wärmepumpe. Herzstück ist unsere KI-gestützte Plattform Enpal.One+, die tausende Systeme intelligent vernetzt und Strombezug sowie -einspeisung effizient am Energiemarkt optimiert.
Bist Du bereit für Lösungen, die mehr als nur ein Versprechen sind und täglich echte Lebensqualität in tausende Haushalte bringen? Was du bei Enpal gestaltest, liefert morgen sauberen Strom und verändert nachhaltig, wie wir Energie nutzen.
Deine Aufgaben
Als Hardware Product Manager (w/m/d) im Bereich Photovoltaik & Heimspeicher bei Enpal entwickelst und skalierst Du innovative Energieprodukte, von Batteriespeichern bis zu Wechselrichtern - und trägst damit aktiv dazu bei, die Energiewende für tausende Haushalte voranzutreiben.
Produktverantwortung mit Tiefe: Du begleitest neue Produkte von der ersten Idee über Discovery, Business Case und Pilotierung bis hin zum erfolgreichen Rollout und der Skalierung.
Strategisches Produktdenken: Du analysierst Markttrends, Nutzerbedürfnisse und technische Anforderungen und entwickelst daraus eine überzeugende Produktstrategie.
Technologie & Business vereinen: Du arbeitest mit Hardware-Ingenieur:innen, Softwareentwickler:innen, den Operations Teams und Lieferanten – und denkst dabei immer aus Kundensicht.
Go-to-Market mit End-to-End-Verantwortung: Du denkst Produkte ganzheitlich mit Fokus auf Markteinführung, Kundenintegration und die organisatorische Skalierung.
Datenbasierte Weiterentwicklung: Du misst den Erfolg Deiner Produkte anhand relevanter KPIs, identifizierst Optimierungspotenziale und leitest konkrete Maßnahmen für die Weiterentwicklung und Skalierung ab.
Dein Profil
2–4 Jahre Erfahrung im Consulting, Produktmanagement, Projektmanagement oder einem schnelllebigen Scale-up-Umfeld optimalerweise mit Hardware Fokus.
Exzellenter Studienabschluss in Ingenieurwesen, Wirtschafts- oder Naturwissenschaften
Technisches Grundverständnis - oder die Bereitschaft, es dir anzueignen. Du musst kein:e Ingenieur:in sein, aber du willst Technik nicht nur verstehen, sondern mitgestalten.
Du übernimmst Verantwortung, treibst Themen eigenständig voran und schaffst es, unterschiedliche Stakeholder für gemeinsame Ziele zu gewinnen.
Deutsch und Englisch fließend (C1+)
Du bist interessiert, auch wenn du nicht alle Anforderungen erfüllst? Bewirb dich trotzdem! Wir freuen uns darauf, dein Potenzial zu entdecken - unabhängig davon, ob deine Erfahrung jede einzelne Voraussetzung erfüllt. ☀️
Darauf kannst Du Dich freuen
Arbeite in Deutschlands erstem grünen Unicorn - Gestalte aktiv die solare Energiewende mit.
Die Sonne scheint auf der ganzen Welt - Bei Enpal arbeitest du mit einem hochmotivierten, diversen Team aus über 65 Nationalitäten.
Das ideale Setup für deinen Fokus - Wir sind überzeugt, dass exzellente Ergebnisse dort entstehen, wo die Bedingungen stimmen. Deshalb setzen wir auf ein hybrides Arbeitskonzept, das dir die Freiheit gibt, immer genau so zu arbeiten, wie es am besten zu deinen Aufgaben passt. Du brauchst neue Impulse auf internationalem Niveau? Mit Workflex bieten wir, abhängig von deiner Verantwortung, die Möglichkeit, deine Tätigkeit bis zu 30 Tage im Jahr ins Ausland zu verlegen.
Grown-up Business & Startup Spirit - In unserem modernen Office in Berlin-Friedrichshain findest du von höhenverstellbaren Tischen, Tischtennisplatte bis gefüllten Getränkekühlschränken und Barista-Coffee alles was das Herz begehrt.
Dein Kick-start bei Enpal - Onboarding-Tag mit Welcome Bag, Buddy-Programm und einem Team, das dich wirklich abholt.
Bleib auf dem Laufenden & gestalte mit - Transparente All-Hands, kurze Entscheidungswege und eine offene Feedback-Kultur, denn ohne Fehler gibt es keinen Fortschritt.
Energiewende geht nur gemeinsam - Bei Enpal erwartet dich ein legen
summary: The role of Hardware Product Manager at Enpal involves developing and scaling innovative energy products, such as batteries and inverters, to drive the energy transition for thousands of households. The ideal candidate will have 2-4 years of experience in consulting, product management, or a similar field, with a strong technical understanding and excellent communication skills. The company offers a dynamic work environment, flexible working hours, and opportunities for professional growth and development.

title: Product Lead
jd_text:
This isn’t your regular job. Almedia is a place where those who want to push harder can accelerate their careers faster than anywhere else. We’re aiming to become Germany’s second bootstrapped unicorn. Almedia is already Europe’s #3 fastest-growing company in 2025 (FT1000).
We are building the future of marketing by rewarding our community of over 70 million users for engaging with our advertisers’ products. We are offering a new way to acquire users for the biggest companies in the world.
Product Manager
€120K – €160K
Offers Equity
Freecash is the product. Almedia is the engagement engine behind it: the smartest way for mobile game studios to turn high-potential users into high-value ones. We're the leader in our category, profitable, and expanding fast.
We're hiring one exceptional Product Leader. Not a fixed lane: real scope, real ownership, and a path to leading a team. If you think like a founder and can't sleep until the data tells you why, keep reading.
What You'll Own
End-to-end outcomes, not tickets. Hypothesis, roadmap, build, metrics, result: you own all of it. You find the highest-impact bets before anyone asks, and you execute. Discovery and delivery are the same job.
The hard, ambiguous problems. Form a sharp point of view, validate against the data, ship with conviction, kill it honestly when the numbers disagree.
Scope that grows. We point you at the biggest opportunity. As you earn it, that expands across Freecash and into new verticals.
What You'll Bring
You think like a founder. You make the call with incomplete information, hold a strong opinion about what will work and why, then prove or disprove it. You don't wait to be told what matters.
You live in the data. You dig in yourself, follow the thread, and come back with the insight nobody else found. Data tells you what to solve. Creativity decides how. Comfortable in Amplitude, Hex, and Looker.
You prioritize ruthlessly. Anyone can list a hundred things. You pick the few that matter right now.
You execute. Ideation to launch to iteration. You bring developers, data, design, and BD with you without slowing down.
You know consumer products. Built and shipped consumer-facing apps.
What Puts You Ahead
You've been a founder. You know what it means to own an outcome with no one to hand it off to.
You've scaled through hyper-growth. 50 to 200+ people, building the plane mid-flight: familiar territory.
Mobile gaming or advertising background. You know the players, the incentives, the dynamics.
Why Almedia?
Own Our Growth: We offer all Berlin-based employees equity in Almedia to truly be a part of our success.
Scale With Almedia: Grow alongside a startup that has been profitable from day one.
Central Berlin Office: Work from a fully-stocked modern office built for collaboration, accessible from all around Berlin.
Other Benefits: Transport subsidy, breakfasts and lunches, language learning, Urban Sports Club, and more.
We believe in fostering talent, evaluating all skill levels during the hiring process, and providing a clear path for growth. Almedia is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace and celebrate diversity, and encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply.
summary: Almedia is seeking a Product Manager to lead the development of their product Freecash, with a focus on end-to-end outcomes, hypothesis-driven roadmaps, and data-driven decision making. The ideal candidate will have a strong product mindset, ability to prioritize ruthlessly, and experience in consumer-facing apps. The role offers a competitive salary, equity, and opportunities for growth in a fast-paced and profitable company.
match_explanation: The job description does not match the user's must-have keywords, but it does mention roadmap planning, which is a nice-to-have keyword.

title: Product Manager Integration (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Jede Geschichte zählt. Gestalte deine eigene – gemeinsam mit uns bei Riverty.
Und wohin führt dich das?
In eine unserer 30 hybriden Arbeitsumgebungen – gestaltet für den Austausch von Ideen, gemeinsames Lernen und die Mitgestaltung unserer Arbeitsweise. Werde Teil einer internationalen Community mit über 4.000 Menschen aus fast 80 Nationen in 11 Ländern. Vereint durch eine gemeinsame Mission: Mit Empathie, moderner Technologie und datengestützten Erkenntnissen dafür zu sorgen, dass Menschen und Unternehmen im Fluss bleiben. Mit Zahlungsprozessen, die für sie erledigt werden – damit sie sich darum keine Sorgen machen müssen.
Und das ist noch nicht alles:
Wir sind Teil der Bertelsmann-Gruppe, die in Familienbesitz ist. Etabliert. Unternehmerisch. In einer dynamischen Branche. Wir ermöglichen flexible Bezahloptionen in verschiedenen Branchen, vereinfachen das Finanzmanagement bekannter Marken und unterstützen Menschen dabei, Schulden abzubauen und finanzielle Sicherheit zu gewinnen. Kurz gesagt: Wir gestalten FinTech.
Wir suchen Unterstützung als
Product Manager Integration (m/w/d)
(befristet, Vollzeit) Schließe dich unserem Team an unserem Standort in Berlin, Verl oder Baden-Baden an – flexible Arbeitsbedingungen verfügbar
Als Product Manager übernimmst du die Verantwortung für einen der kritischsten Hebel unseres Wachstums:
Die schnelle, skalierbare und standardisierte Integration neuer Kund:innen auf unsere Plattform. Mit zunehmender Digitalisierung vernetzen wir uns stärker mit unseren Kund:Innen. Anstelle kundenspezifischer Lösungen wollen wir mit wiederverwendbaren Bausteinen Synergieeffekte heben und die Grundlage für weitere Automatisierungen in unseren Kundenbeziehungen schaffen.
Deine Aufgabe ist es, daraus ein skalierbares, klar strukturiertes System mit Produktcharakter zu entwickeln. Du bewegst dich dabei an der Schnittstelle von Produkt, Plattform, Operations und Kundenteams.
Das sind deine Verantwortungsbereiche:
Integration & Onboarding skalierbar machen
Du entwickelst standardisierte Komponenten, Templates und Konfigurationslösungen, damit neue Integrationen auf unserer Plattform schnell, automatisch und effizient umgesetzt werden können
Abhängigkeiten reduzieren
Du sorgst dafür, dass interne Teams, Konfigurationen eigenständig und zuverlässig umsetzen können, indem du Standards definierst, deren Einsatz schulst und reviewst. Damit sorgst du für schnelle Rollouts und einen stabilen Betrieb unserer Lösungen
Plattform-Standards vorantreiben
Du definierst gemeinsam mit anderen Produktteams klare Standards für Integrationen und stellst sicher, dass diese im Alltag gelebt werden
Systembausteine verstehen und mitgestalten
Du kennst bestehende Systembausteine und weißt diese in konkreten Situationen anzuwenden
Du vermittelst zwischen Anforderungen und bestehenden Lösungen und gestaltest diese mit
Teams verbinden
Du arbeitest eng mit Produkt-, Technik-, Operations- und kundenverantwortlichen Teams zusammen und sorgst für klare Prioritäten
Das bringst du mit:
Du verfügst über sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse (C1+)
Du hast mehrjährige Erfahrung (5+ Jahre) in leitenden Produktrollen, idealerweise im Inkasso-, B2B-SaaS-, FinTech- oder regulierten Umfeld, und weißt, wie man kundenzentrierte Lösungen erfolgreich umsetzt
Du bist sicher im Umgang mit großen Unternehmenskunden und bewegst dich souverän in komplexen Stakeholder-Strukturen
Du hast Erfahrung im Aufbau und in der Führung cross-funktionaler Teams und arbeitest routiniert mit agilen Methoden, um Zusammenarbeit und Leistungsfähigkeit zu stärken
Du bringst ein gutes Verständnis für Systemlandschaften mit und hast Freude daran, bestehende Plattformen zu modernisieren und in skalierbare, zukunftsfähige Architekturen zu überführen
Equal Opportunity Employer Statement
Wir wollen ein fairer und inclusiver Arbeitgeber sein. Wir schätzen die unterschiedlichen Perspektiven, die eine vielfältige Belegschaft einbringt. Daher suchen wir aktiv nach Menschen, die unser Unter
summary: The Product Manager Integration role at Riverty involves developing a scalable and standardized system for integrating new customers onto the company's platform, working with cross-functional teams, and defining standards for integrations. The ideal candidate has experience in leading product roles, preferably in the FinTech or B2B SaaS industry, and is skilled in working with large enterprise customers and complex stakeholder structures. title: Product Manager – Analytics Platform (f/m/d)
jd_text:
Über Uns
Do you want to take ownership from day one and actively shape the future in a growing company? VoiceLine is a fast-growing, VC-backed startup based in Munich. We build an AI-powered solution that helps field sales and service teams turn conversations into structured data efficiently. This enables customer information to be documented, analyzed, and seamlessly integrated into existing systems. Together with leading enterprise customers, we are working to make field sales future ready.
Deine Aufgaben
Own and evolve the Analytics & Coaching Platform
Take over full ownership of the Analytics Platform roadmap from the CPO.
Develop a deep understanding of how sales organisations work, how they measure performance, and what good revenue intelligence looks like.
Talk directly with senior sales executives and revenue operations leaders at our customers to understand their BI requirements and translate them into clear, well-prioritised product cases.
Steer the product tribe around the Analytics Platform as it grows.
Drive product operations end-to-end
Collect, structure, and prioritise customer feedback from Sales, Customer Success, and direct customer interactions.
Translate feedback and opportunities into well-specified features ready for engineering.
Ensure clean handoffs from product decisions through development to release.
Align teams and communicate product
Prepare and communicate releases internally and externally — to go-to-market, engineering, and customers.
Act as a connector between Product, Engineering, Customer Success, and Sales, ensuring shared context and minimal friction.
Drive alignment proactively, without relying on hierarchy.
Partner with the CPO across product initiatives
Act as a close operational partner to the CPO, contributing to cross-cutting product initiatives beyond the Analytics Platform.
Maintain clear product documentation and drive continuous improvement of product processes.
Dein Profil
We are open to two distinct backgrounds for this role — what matters is that you bring strong product instincts, a deep understanding of sales organisations, and the communication skills to work with senior stakeholders:
Product management background: You have 2–4 years of experience in a product role, ideally in B2B SaaS or enterprise software. You know how to run discovery, write specs, prioritise a backlog, and work with engineering. You are looking for a role where you can own a product area end-to-end from day one.
Commercial or sales background: You have built your career in sales, field sales, revenue operations, or a similar commercial role. You have worked hands-on with sales dashboards and BI tools, driven revenue performance, and understand from the inside how sales organisations operate. You are now ready to transition into product management and bring that frontline expertise with you.
Across both profiles, we are looking for someone who:
Has a deep understanding of how sales organisations work — pipelines, forecasting, coaching, revenue intelligence — either from working in them or building products for them.
Is an outstanding communicator — outspoken, structured, and confident talking with senior executives. You can run a customer discovery conversation and come out with clear, actionable requirements.
Has a basic understanding of how software is built — you understand concepts like APIs, data models, and development cycles well enough to work closely with engineers, ask the right questions, and write clear specifications.
Is analytically minded and data-fluent — comfortable with BI concepts and able to define what good analytics look like for a sales organisation.
Is ownership-oriented — you take full responsibility for outcomes, drive decisions without waiting for direction, and improve things when they are unclear or inefficient.
Uses AI-first workflows and is comfortable with tools like Claude, ChatGPT, Notion AI, or automation platforms to move faster and do more.
Is business-fluent in both
summary: The role is for a product manager to own and evolve the Analytics & Coaching Platform, driving product operations end-to-end and partnering with the CPO across product initiatives. The ideal candidate has a deep understanding of sales organisations and strong product instincts, with experience in B2B SaaS or enterprise software. The role requires outstanding communication skills, analytical mindset, and data fluency, with the ability to work closely with engineers and senior stakeholders.

title: Product Manager, CRM (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Purpose of Position
The Salesforce Product Manager combines strong product and operational process expertise with creative problem-solving, translating complex commercial needs into scalable solutions in line with the CRM roadmap and vision. Working in a fast-paced, global environment, this role brings teams together, leads stakeholders across functional while driving meaningful outcomes in a complex and evolving systems landscape.
Key Tasks
Act as primary product linkage between business stakeholders, the Salesforce team, and Product & Technology, ensuring business priorities are clearly understood and reflected in the CRM roadmap.
Support the definition, maintenance, and delivery of the CRM roadmap, working closely with CRM and Billing & Payments leadership and teams, to surface synergies and address cross-team dependencies early.
Lead structured assessments of current (“as-is”) business processes and contribute to the definition of future (“to-be”) processes, helping identify opportunities, benefits and impacts of proposed changes.
Translate commercial, contractual, and operational needs, challenge assumptions and translate them into clear, actionable product requirements that support effective delivery within a complex systems landscape.
Work closely with the Salesforce Business Partners and engineering teams to ensure solutions align with agreed process models and integrate effectively across platforms.
Support scalable and well governed CRM data foundations that enable reliable reporting, analytics, and downstream use cases.
Drive change and adoption by promoting new ways of working, supporting users through transitions, and helping teams realise value from the CRM platform.
Clearly communicate priorities, progress, trade-offs, and outcomes to a broad set of stakeholders through strong narratives and data-backed decision making.
Skills & Expertise
3+ years of product management experience, with hands-on involvement in CRM products, Salesforce experience preferred.
Strong product mindset, with the ability to frame problems, make trade-offs, and keep teams focused on outcomes in complex system landscape.
Experience translating complex business, contractual, or operational needs into clear product requirements, roadmaps, and prioritized backlogs.
Data-driven approach, using data to inform decisions while applying sound business judgement where information is incomplete.
Demonstrated analytical and problem-solving skills, able to identify gaps and contribute practical, scalable solutions to cross-functional challenges.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to create clear product narratives, explain trade-offs, and support stakeholders through change.
Confidence working with and influencing a broad set of stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams in a global environment.
Adaptability to changing priorities and environments.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex information.
Client-oriented mindset with a focus on delivering value.
Experience working in cross-functional teams and engaging stakeholders plus strong negotiation and influencing skills.
Our Offer
Flexi-Week: We prioritise your mental health and wellbeing by offering you a four-day Flexi-Week (with one lighter or completely disconnected day per week) at full pay, with no reduction to your annual holiday allowance.
Flexi-office: We offer an international culture and flexibility through our hybrid/remote working scheme which is designed to foster a culture of mutual trust and working flexibility.
Work Expense Contribution & Remote Working Furniture: You will receive a monthly allowance to cover part of your running costs, as well as a furniture package to support you in setting up a comfortable workspace when working from home.
Health and Wellbeing: With our support and access to various initiatives and sports offers, you can focus on your mental and physical wellbeing.
Development: We’ve built our extensive training suite
summary: The Salesforce Product Manager role combines product and operational process expertise to translate complex commercial needs into scalable solutions, working in a fast-paced global environment to drive meaningful outcomes. The role involves acting as a primary product linkage between stakeholders, supporting the definition and delivery of the CRM roadmap, and leading structured assessments of business processes. The ideal candidate will have strong product management experience, a data-driven approach, and excellent communication skills.

title: Product Manager – Platform Team (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Why Us?
DocuWare stands for globally distributed, international teams and an open corporate culture that invites you to help shape it. Mobile work and flexible working hours are part of our everyday life. Would you like to be part of an innovative company whose solutions are digitizing everyday work in a wide range of industries? In that case, we are happy to welcome you to the team.
Your Responsibilities
As a Product Manager – Platform Team (m/f/d), you shape the foundational platform, cloud operations, integrations, and base services that all DocuWare AI Hub teams depend on. The AI Hub platform operates NLP, Computer Vision, and Large Language Models at scale – served via inference infrastructure such as NVIDIA Triton. Treating internal engineering teams as your customers, you ensure they have a reliable, scalable, and developer-friendly foundation to build upon – while acting as the team's spokesperson in OKR planning, roadmap reviews, and cross-team alignment.
Your responsibilities
You define and continuously refine the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for the platform, cloud operations, integrations, and base services – prioritizing investments, evaluating build vs. buy decisions, and driving adoption across all AI Hub teams while building business cases that demonstrate ROI through productivity gains, cost savings, and reliability improvements.
Through continuous user research with AI Hub engineering teams, you validate platform improvement ideas, identify and remove friction in developer workflows, and prioritize competing requests – all while assessing new technological opportunities in cloud infrastructure, container orchestration, CI/CD, observability, and integration patterns to enhance self-service capabilities and developer autonomy.
You coordinate the timely, high-quality delivery of platform increments in an interdisciplinary, agile team – working closely with Engineering and Machine Learning to clarify requirements, resolve blockers, and support technical decision-making, while constructively challenging complexity and fostering an iterative, data-driven development approach with fast learning cycles.
You define and monitor platform success metrics – including deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, developer satisfaction, and cloud spend efficiency – driving cost optimization strategies and deriving concrete improvement initiatives from data, experiments, and developer feedback.
As the spokesperson for the Platform Team, you communicate strategy, roadmap, and progress transparently to stakeholders and leadership – translating technical work and debt into tangible business outcomes, driving adoption through evangelism and change management, and building consensus across a matrix organization by influencing without authority.
What You Need To Succeed
You hold a degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Business Informatics, or a comparable field, combined with several years of Product Management experience in platform engineering, DevOps, or infrastructure products.
You bring a strong platform engineering mindset – treating internal developers as customers – with a proven track record in roadmapping, building business cases, and driving platform adoption through research, evangelism, and change management.
You have conceptual knowledge of cloud platforms (Azure or equivalent), Kubernetes, container orchestration, and infrastructure-as-code, CI/CD (e.g., GitLab), observability patterns, and integration architectures (event handling, messaging, shared APIs), as well as operational awareness of AI/ML model serving and inference infrastructure (e.g., NVIDIA Triton) – you do not need to train models, but you should understand the operational challenges of running them at scale.
You have a strong sense for technical feasibility, with the ability to challenge complexity, make data-driven decisions, and navigate ambiguity.
Your exceptional communication skills enable you to convey platform value to both tech
summary: The Product Manager – Platform Team role at DocuWare involves shaping the foundational platform, cloud operations, integrations, and base services for AI Hub teams, ensuring a reliable and scalable foundation for internal engineering teams. The role requires defining and refining the product vision, strategy, and roadmap, as well as coordinating the delivery of platform increments and defining success metrics. The ideal candidate has a strong platform engineering mindset, experience in roadmapping and driving platform adoption, and conceptual knowledge of cloud platforms, Kubernetes, and infrastructure-as-code.

title: Product Owner / Product Manager (all genders)
jd_text:
Deine Aufgaben
Kunden verstehen & Produkt gestalten
Du arbeitest eng mit deutschsprachigen Kunden, Partnern, Beta-Kunden und internen Stakeholdern zusammen, um Herausforderungen, Anforderungen und neue Chancen zu identifizieren.
Du führst qualitative und quantitative Kundeninterviews, Discovery-Gespräche, Workshops und Anforderungsanalysen durch.
Du hörst sehr gut zu, stellst die richtigen Fragen und erkennst auch unausgesprochene Kundenbedürfnisse.
Du übersetzt Kundenfeedback, Interview-Ergebnisse, Marktanforderungen und Geschäftsziele in klare Produktentscheidungen, Roadmap-Themen und priorisierte Anforderungen.
Du arbeitest mit Beta-Kunden an frühen Produktversionen, testest Hypothesen, sammelst strukturiertes Feedback und leitest daraus konkrete Verbesserungen ab.
Du validierst Annahmen frühzeitig und stellst sicher, dass wir die richtigen Probleme lösen, bevor Entwicklungskapazität investiert wird.
Technische Produktentwicklung steuern
Du verantwortest und priorisierst den Product Backlog – nicht nur aus Kundensicht, sondern auch mit Blick auf technische Machbarkeit, Abhängigkeiten und langfristige Produktqualität.
Du übersetzt Anforderungen in klare User Stories, Akzeptanzkriterien und technische Kontexte, mit denen Engineering sinnvoll arbeiten kann.
Du arbeitest sehr eng mit Backend Engineers, Data Science und Fullstack Development zusammen – von der ersten Idee bis zum produktiven Einsatz beim Kunden.
Du bist aktiv in Refinements, Sprint Plannings, Daily Standups und Release-Prozesse eingebunden.
Du kannst technische Diskussionen mitführen, Fragen stellen, Annahmen hinterfragen und Begriffe wie APIs, Endpunkte, Payloads oder Postman sicher einordnen.
Du sorgst dafür, dass Features mit Klarheit, Qualität und messbarem Mehrwert ausgeliefert werden.
Priorisieren, entscheiden & Klarheit schaffen
Du bringst eine echte Produktperspektive ein und verstehst dich nicht als reine Koordinationsfunktion.
Du hinterfragst Anforderungen, erkennst Zielkonflikte und triffst fundierte Entscheidungen auf Basis von Kundennutzen, Geschäftswert und technischer Umsetzbarkeit.
Du schaffst Klarheit zwischen Kunden, Geschäftsführung und Engineering und sorgst dafür, dass alle Beteiligten verstehen, warum wir etwas bauen – oder bewusst nicht bauen.
Du hilfst dem Team, fokussiert zu bleiben und High-Impact-Features schnell und pragmatisch auszuliefern.
Nutzererlebnis & Produktqualität verbessern
Du denkst über Features hinaus und beschäftigst dich mit Workflows, Nutzererlebnis und Bedienbarkeit.
Du hinterfragst Komplexität und suchst nach Möglichkeiten, anspruchsvolle technische Prozesse für Anwender:innen einfacher und verständlicher zu machen.
Du arbeitest mit Mockups, Prototypen und KI-gestützten Tools, um Ideen schnell zu testen und zu validieren.
Du stellst sicher, dass unsere technisch anspruchsvollen Lösungen für Kunden intuitiv nutzbar bleiben.
Erfolg messen & Produkt weiterentwickeln
Du definierst Erfolgsmetriken für neue Features und Produktinitiativen.
Du kombinierst qualitative Insights aus Kundeninterviews mit quantitativen Daten aus Nutzung, Feedback und Produktkennzahlen.
Du validierst neue Funktionen anhand von Beta-Kunden, Nutzerfeedback, Experimenten und – wo sinnvoll – A/B-Tests.
Du arbeitest aktiv an der Weiterentwicklung unserer Produktstrategie und Roadmap mit.
Dein Profil
Mindestens 3 Jahre Erfahrung als Product Owner, Product Manager, Technical Product Manager oder in einer vergleichbaren Produktrolle.
Verhandlungssicheres Deutsch ist ein absolutes Muss, da du regelmäßig mit deutschsprachigen Kunden arbeitest. Gute Englischkenntnisse sind für die Zusammenarbeit im internationalen Team erforderlich.
